title: Add Two Numbers Within 10
description: Compute the sum of two whole numbers whose total is within 10.
condition: Given two whole numbers within 10 to be added (e.g., a + b).
response: State the correct sum a + b.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Fluency may be based on counting on or known facts.

title: Find Difference Within 10
description: Determine how many more or how many are left by subtracting within 10.
condition: Given two whole numbers within 10 where one is compared to or taken from the other (a − b or compare a and b).
label: Production / Skill
notes: May be solved by counting back or counting on.

title: Interpret Equal Sign as Same Value
description: Understand that the equal sign means both sides of an equation represent the same value.
condition: Given an equation of the form expression = expression.
response: Interpret or ensure that both sides have the same total value.
label: Concept
example: In $5 + 4 = 9$, both sides name the same number, 9.
notes: Supports understanding of operations on either side of =.

title: Match Addition Equation to Story (Add To/Put Together)
description: Select or write an addition equation that represents an add-to or put-together story situation.
condition: Given a story problem describing combining quantities with a known total or unknown addend.
response: Write or select an equation of the form a + b = c that matches the quantities in the story.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Story: 6 footballs and some baseballs make 10. Equation: $6 + \Box = 10$.
notes: Requires mapping story quantities to equation positions.

title: Match Subtraction Equation to Story
description: Select or write a subtraction equation that represents a take-from or compare story situation.
condition: Given a story problem describing taking away or comparing two quantities.
response: Write or select an equation of the form a − b = c that matches the story.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Story: 8 shapes total, 2 are stars. Equation: $8 - 2 = \Box$.

title: Represent Difference Using Objects or Drawings
description: Use visual models (e.g., circles) to determine how many more or how many remain.
condition: Given a visual representation of two quantities.
response: Count remaining or extra objects to determine the difference.
label: Production / Skill
example: From 8 red and 5 yellow circles, 3 red circles are extra, so $8 - 5 = 3$.

title: Solve for Unknown Addend
description: Determine the missing number in an addition equation with one unknown addend.
condition: Given an equation of the form a + □ = c or □ + a = c within 10.
response: State the value of the unknown addend.
label: Production / Skill
notes: May use counting on or related subtraction.
